Definition: A step-by-step procedure or set of rules designed to solve a problem efficiently, focusing on minimizing time and space complexity.
A method of organizing and storing data to enable efficient access and modification, such as arrays, lists, and trees.
A named reference to a memory location that stores a value which can change during program execution.
A classification that specifies the type of value a variable holds and the operations that can be performed on it.
A whole number data type without a fractional component, used for counting and indexing.
A numeric data type that represents decimal or real numbers with precision limitations.
A sequence of characters used to represent text data.
A data type that represents one of two values: True or False, commonly used in logic and conditions.
An ordered, mutable collection that allows duplicate elements and dynamic resizing.
An ordered, immutable collection used for fixed data that should not change.
A collection of key-value pairs that allows fast retrieval of values using unique keys.
An unordered collection of unique elements useful for eliminating duplicates.
A reusable block of code that performs a specific task and can return a result.
A parameter is a variable in a function definition, while an argument is the actual value passed during a function call.
A statement that exits a function and optionally sends a value back to the caller.
Controls the flow of execution based on whether a condition evaluates to True or False.
A control structure that repeats a block of code multiple times.
A loop used to iterate over a sequence like a list or range.
A loop that continues executing as long as a specified condition is True.
A blueprint for creating objects, defining their attributes and methods.
An instance of a class that contains actual data and behavior.
A function defined inside a class that operates on its objects.
A variable associated with a class or object that stores data.
The practice of bundling data and methods together while restricting direct access to some components.
A feature that allows a class to acquire properties and behavior from another class.
The ability to use a single interface to represent different underlying data types or objects.
The concept of hiding complex implementation details and exposing only essential features.
A technique where a function calls itself to solve smaller instances of a problem.
A Last-In, First-Out data structure used in function calls and memory management.
A First-In, First-Out data structure used in scheduling and data processing.
A collection of elements stored in contiguous memory locations, allowing efficient indexing.
The position of an element within a sequence, starting from zero in Python.
The process of extracting a portion of a sequence using index ranges.
An error that occurs during the execution of a program.
A structure used to handle exceptions and prevent program crashes.
The process of identifying, analyzing, and fixing errors in code.
The set of rules that defines the structure of valid code.
The period during which a program is actively executing.
A program that executes code line-by-line rather than compiling it all at once.
A program that translates source code into machine code before execution.
A mathematical notation used to describe the performance or complexity of an algorithm.
A measure of how the execution time of an algorithm grows with input size.
A measure of how much memory an algorithm uses relative to input size.
An efficient search algorithm that repeatedly divides a sorted dataset in half.
A simple search algorithm that checks each element sequentially.
The process of arranging data in a specific order such as ascending or descending.
A data structure that maps keys to values using a hash function for fast access.
A function that converts input data into a fixed-size value used for indexing.
A situation where two inputs produce the same hash value.
A collection of nodes and edges used to represent relationships.
A hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes with parent-child relationships.
A linear data structure where elements are stored in nodes connected by pointers.
A variable that stores the memory address of another variable.
The hardware component where data and programs are stored during execution.
The smallest unit of execution within a process.
An instance of a program that is being executed.
The ability to manage multiple tasks at once.
The simultaneous execution of multiple tasks.
A set of rules that allows different software systems to communicate.
An organized collection of structured data for efficient storage and retrieval.
